risks: Card ordering with concurrent drag-and-drop: two users moving cards simultaneously can cause rank collisions — requires fractional indexing (e.g., LexoRank) or server-authoritative ordering with conflict resolution

Real-time sync architecture choice (WebSockets via Socket.io vs. server-sent events vs. third-party like Pusher/Ably) affects horizontal scalability — sticky sessions or a pub/sub broker (Redis) needed if running multiple server instances
Board load performance degrades as card count grows — need to decide upfront whether to paginate cards per column or lazy-load, since this affects both API design and frontend rendering strategy
Notification fan-out: @mentions or card updates on high-membership workspaces could trigger hundreds of simultaneous notifications — requires a job queue (e.g., BullMQ) rather than inline processing
features: Kanban board with drag-and-drop cards across customizable columns
Real-time board updates when teammates move or edit cards
Task cards with title, description, assignee, due date, labels, and attachments
Team workspaces with role-based permissions (Owner, Admin, Member, Viewer)
Activity log per card and per board
Assignee filtering and label-based filtering on boards
Board templates for common workflows (Bug Tracking, Sprint, Content Pipeline)
Search across cards within a workspace
userFlows: User registers → verifies email → creates Workspace → invited teammates receive email → teammates accept invite and join Workspace
Member opens Board → drags card from 'In Progress' to 'Done' → all teammates on the same board see card move in real-time without page refresh
Member creates Card → sets due date → assigns teammate → assignee receives in-app and email notification → assignee opens card from notification
Member tries to delete a Column that still contains 10 cards → warning prompt shows card count → user must choose: move all cards to another column OR confirm force-delete which archives cards
Admin invites user via email → invite expires after 7 days → expired invite clicked → user sees expiry message → Admin must resend invite
Viewer role user attempts to drag a card → drag is disabled → tooltip explains read-only access
Member searches for a card by keyword → results show matching cards with their board and column context → clicking result opens card detail modal
constraints: Drag-and-drop reorder must persist card position within 500ms of drop (optimistic UI with server reconciliation)
Real-time updates must propagate to all connected board viewers within 1 second under normal network conditions
API responses under 300ms p95 for board load (all columns and cards fetched in single request)
Workspace invite links expire after 7 days
Attachment file size limit of 10MB per file
Board must remain usable (read-only fallback) if WebSocket connection drops
Column and card ordering must be deterministic — stored as fractional indexing or integer rank to avoid full re-rank on every move
description: A team-based project management tool centered on Kanban boards for visualizing workflow stages. Supports multi-user collaboration with real-time updates, task assignment, and progress tracking. Valuable for distributed teams needing a lightweight alternative to tools like Trello or Linear without per-seat pricing complexity.
mvpFeatures: Kanban board with drag-and-drop cards and fixed columns (To Do, In Progress, Done)
User authentication and workspace creation with invite-by-email
Task cards with title, description, assignee, and due date
Role-based access: Owner and Member only
openQuestions: Is real-time collaboration scoped to seeing card movements only, or does it include live co-editing of card descriptions (latter requires CRDT or OT and significantly changes architecture)?
Are boards scoped to Projects which are scoped to Workspaces (3-tier hierarchy), or do users want a flat list of boards per Workspace — this changes navigation UX and data model depth?
Should notifications be strictly in-app and email, or is Slack/webhook integration required in v1 — this determines whether a notification abstraction layer is needed from day one?
entityRelationships: User N:N Workspace (via WorkspaceMember with RoleEnum)
